title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace are collaborating on a 2028 demonstration mission to test air-breathing propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) imaging. The mission aims to showcase geospatial capabilities using innovative propulsion technology.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites has become a critical weapon in the US-Iran conflict, marking a shift toward geospatial warfare. The distinction between civilian and military satellite data is increasingly blurred.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch several new and reusable rockets in the coming weeks and months, including both state-led and commercial efforts. These debuts and first-stage recovery attempts mark significant advancements in the country's space program.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test at Cape Canaveral on May 28, destroying the rocket and causing significant pad damage.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime attacks. These facilities aim to enhance the resilience and survivability of critical space operations.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Setting up a permanent lunar presence needs investment in biology
summary: Establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon requires significant investment in biological research. The Apollo 17 mission in 1972 marked the last time humans walked on the lunar surface.

title: Revolv Space enters in-orbit servicing market with Infinite Orbits deal
summary: Revolv Space has entered the in-orbit servicing market by securing a deal with Infinite Orbits. The agreement involves Revolv Space providing Solar Array Drive Assemblies (SADAs) for Infinite Orbits' satellite operations.

title: Germany pushes European military space command initiative
summary: Germany advocates for a unified European approach to military space operations by proposing a European Space Component Command hosted by Germany. This initiative aims to enhance coordination among European nations in space defense.

title: CEO Series: Lux Aeterna’s Brian Taylor on what’s next for on-orbit servicing
summary: Lux Aeterna's CEO Brian Taylor discusses the challenges of disposable satellites and the potential of on-orbit servicing in a Space Minds interview. The conversation highlights future advancements in satellite maintenance and sustainability.

title: Incredible videos show Blue Origin rocket explosion could be seen from hundreds of miles away
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during testing on May 28, creating a visible display of flames and debris across wide areas of Florida. The incident occurred during a nighttime test, with the explosion visible from hundreds of miles away.

title: SpaceX sends 29 Starlink satellites into orbit on Falcon 9 rocket launch from Florida
summary: SpaceX launched 29 Starlink satellites into orbit using a Falcon 9 rocket from Florida. The mission took place on May 29, 2026, from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: It's so hot across Europe, you can see the heat wave from space | Space photo of the day for May 29, 2026
summary: A heat wave across Europe is visible from space, as captured in a satellite image from May 29, 2026. The extreme temperatures are highlighted by red hues in the satellite data.

title: 'The Man Who Fell To Earth' at 50: Was David Bowie’s strange stranded alien his best role ever?
summary: The article reflects on David Bowie's iconic role as an alien stranded on Earth in the 1976 film 'The Man Who Fell To Earth', directed by Nicolas Roeg. It discusses whether this performance remains Bowie's best role and its lasting impact.

title: Red dwarf stars are cosmic killers that eat their own planets
summary: Astronomers found evidence that red dwarf stars may consume their own planets. This discovery highlights the destructive potential of these common cosmic objects.

title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes in massive fireball during prelaunch test
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a prelaunch engine test, potentially delaying the company's progress and NASA's moon missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the aerospace firm.

title: Companies like SpaceX want electromagnetic catapults on the moon. Could they be used as weapons?
summary: Companies like SpaceX are exploring the use of electromagnetic catapults on the Moon, but a new report warns these could be repurposed as first-strike weapons. The report highlights the strategic and security risks of deploying such technology.

title: Andy Weir's 'The Martian' just got a deluxe edition to celebrate the book's 15-year anniversary, and it's a stunner
summary: Andy Weir's 'The Martian' receives a deluxe edition to celebrate its 15-year anniversary, featuring significant enhancements. The book, which predates 'Project Hail Mary,' is being re-released as a visually impressive edition.

title: NASA is hatching a 'fast-paced plan' to boost this space telescope. But first, they'll have to find it
summary: NASA is planning to boost the orbit of its Swift space telescope using a private spacecraft, requiring precise predictions of its location this fall. The mission aims to extend the telescope's operational lifespan by increasing its altitude.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), which is located approximately 63 million light-years away. This galaxy is part of the Virgo Cluster and is moving toward its center.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa reveals crop types and seasonal changes using a vivid color palette. The imagery highlights variations in the Southern Hemisphere's growing season.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is set to begin a new phase of test flights, including its first supersonic flight. The milestone flights will test mission-critical objectives for the unique aircraft.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs discusses potential challenges for Artemis human landing systems due to lunar dust clouds during Moon landings. The issue is compared to driving through dust and dirt that obscures visibility.

title: New Landsat Science Team Holds First In-Person Meeting
summary: The new Landsat Science Team held its first in-person meeting from May 5 to 7 at the EROS Center in Sioux Falls, SD. The event introduced the 2026–2030 Science Team members.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars. The achievement was confirmed through downlink data analyzed by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory team.

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: Scientists analyzed 20 Martian samples from NASA’s Curiosity Rover and found that hematite crystallite size variations could indicate ancient climate changes on Mars.

title: Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope Poster
summary: NASA released high-resolution posters and images of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The materials are available for download in various formats.

title: Contractor to Civil Servant: NASA Welcomes Kenny Heckle
summary: NASA welcomes Kenny Heckle, who grew up near Kennedy Space Center and has a background in union pipefitting and fabrication. The article highlights his personal connection to NASA and his family's history in the trade.

title: NASA Astronaut Andrew Morgan Retires
summary: NASA astronaut Andrew Morgan retired from the agency on May 28, 2026, after a 12-year career, including 272 days in space aboard the ISS. He joined NASA's 21st astronaut class in 2013 and transitioned to continue his military service.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic explosion during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative rebrands existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of its Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four of them being SpaceX's Starlink missions. Amazon's Leo satellite missions are also part of the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 2022. This marks another mission to sustain and expand operations aboard the orbital outpost.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3 upgrades, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from an unspecified location. The mission marked a significant milestone for SpaceX's Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as the 'booster of the future' for SpaceX's next-generation launch system.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet without additional context or details.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on five decades of contributions to space exploration and science.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these researchers.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to advance training, operational efficiency, and public engagement in space exploration. This technology is being used to create immersive simulation environments and enhance mission simulations.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test, likely related to a spacecraft landing system, was conducted. The 'bake-out' suggests a thermal test to ensure the parachute's performance under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed to improve the handling and retrieval of space debris and satellites. This innovation could significantly enhance space operations and safety.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic failure during its launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitions in the space industry.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at the SmallSat Europe event. The gathering emphasized the continent's advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has raised $90 million in a Series A funding round to expand its laser and optical hardware production and international presence beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with a new investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel type of propulsion technology. This funding aims to bring this advanced engine design to the commercial space market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech integrated NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into its S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers. This upgrade significantly expands the supercomputer's performance for space-based applications.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for Lunar Terrain Vehicles (LTV) and lunar transport services, signaling a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference led by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), in-space manufacturing, lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself. It suggests that government support is necessary to drive demand and advance the industry.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured an $18M+ defense-monitoring contract, marking its first major agreement following a strategic shift to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for non-Earth imagery services, capturing images of objects in orbit. This highlights the growing demand for space situational awareness and orbital debris monitoring.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: Virgin Media O2's direct-to-device (D2D) service, O2 Satellite, now supports iPhones. The service uses SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 is expanding its leadership team with new roles including a chief commercial officer (CCO) and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships to support global growth. The company, which specializes in satellite communications technology, is positioning itself for international expansion.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity, as highlighted in discussions around SpaceX's IPO documents. The future of moving compute to space is a key topic in the space industry.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is questioning when it will achieve more efficient and reliable access to space, with this issue being a major topic at a recent launch panel. The discussion highlights the urgency for advancements in Europe's launch capabilities.

title: Archangel Lightworks Demonstrates Deployable Optical Ground Station
summary: Archangel Lightworks has successfully tested its deployable optical ground station Terra-M, demonstrating data transfer with a Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite. The field tests validate the ground station's capability to support optical communications in space.

title: Anuvu Taps Eutelsat for IFC Capacity on Eutelsat 10B
summary: Anuvu has signed a multi-year agreement with Eutelsat to use satellite capacity on the Eutelsat 10B satellite for in-flight connectivity (IFC) services. The Eutelsat 10B satellite is positioned at 10 degrees East.

title: European Commission Reserves Portion of Critical MSS Spectrum for EU Operators
summary: The European Commission has outlined a process to allocate licenses for critical mobile satellite services (MSS) spectrum in Europe. The move aims to prioritize EU operators for spectrum usage.

title: Space Force Awards SpaceX $2.3 Billion Contract for Space Data Network
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded SpaceX a $2.29 billion contract to develop the Space Data Network (SDN) Backbone, a proliferated Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) system. This contract aims to enhance secure and resilient data transmission capabilities for military and government applications.
